rationale: Build on the winning row-offset quantizer, but stop paying offset metadata unless it materially lowers actual roundtrip error. For each 2D tensor, this version compares plain per-row int8 against the centered row-offset variant and keeps offsets only when the centered reconstruction cuts MSE by at least 8%. That is a size-aware fidelity gate aligned with the final int8 zlib roundtrip objective, and it should also avoid weak offset insertions that the previous mean-vs-residual heuristic could admit.
title: MSE-Gated Adaptive Row-Offset Int8
